title: Squash & Sage Spelt Risotto
summary: We’ve swapped white risotto rice for spelt in this autumnal squash risotto, giving you four times the amount of fibre and making your gut microbes very happy.

method.description: Cut the squash into chunky wedges and roast in the oven for 35 mins until soft enough to mash with a fork.
Cook the spelt following packet instructions.
Heat the olive oil in a large, deep frying pan over a medium heat and add the sage leaves. Cook for a few mins until they’re crisp, then set aside for garnish and leave the oil in the pan.
Mince the garlic then place in the pan with the sage-infused oil. Fry over a low heat until it turns translucent and smells fragrant.

Remove the cooked squash from the oven and add it to the pan along with the water and spelt. Mix everything together really well until the squash breaks down to form a creamy sauce. Add more water as needed.
Divide the risotto between plates and top with the crispy sage and a sprinkling of Parmesan, if using.

chefs.name: Healthy by ZOE
chefs.summary: ZOE (@ZOE) is a science and nutrition company leading a movement to transform the health of millions through smarter food choices. ZOE means life in Greek — and ZOE passionately believes everyone can feel better and live longer, healthier lives by rethinking what they eat. ZOE conducts the world’s largest nutrition science study to better understand the relationship between food and health. Based on this research, their personalised nutrition program delivers tailored advice based on metabolic and gut health, turning complex science into clear step-by-step actions to improve your health with every meal. Become a member at zoe.com. All of their recipes are developed by nutritionists to help you embrace food that tastes amazing and makes you feel even better.
